moves.note: Add quality tech with strong read

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to eliminate high risk.
Trim semiconductor (SMH) and AI/Software (CRWD) to reduce concentration.
Reduce broad index (SPY) to meet single-name limit and free up cash.
Increase allocation to income ETF (QQQI) for stability and yield.
Add a high-quality tech name (AVGO) with a strong scanner read.
Maintain a significant cash buffer for capital preservation.
conservative.read: This plan aims for capital preservation with a target of around 1.5% monthly return. It significantly reduces risk by cutting leveraged ETFs and trimming concentrated positions, while building a cash buffer.

moves.note: Add quality tech with strong read

plan: Reduce leveraged ETF exposure (SOXL) and exit others (SPAL).
Trim semiconductor (SMH) and broad index (SPY) to lower concentration.
Slightly increase income ETF (QQQI) for a balanced approach.
Add a high-quality tech name (AVGO) with a strong scanner read.
Introduce a new, high-scoring tech setup (NVDU) for growth potential.
Maintain a moderate cash buffer for flexibility.
balanced.read: This plan targets risk-first growth with an estimated 2.5% monthly return. It balances broad index exposure with quality tech and limited leverage, while reducing concentration and maintaining a cash buffer.

plan: Increase allocation to leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) for amplified gains.
Trim semiconductor (SMH) and broad index (SPY) to allow concentration.
Increase conviction in AI/Software (CRWD) for growth.
Add the highest-rated scanner setups (GUSH, NVDU) for strong potential.
Maintain a smaller cash buffer to maximize invested capital.
Utilize wider but strict stops to manage amplified risk.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets stretched growth with an estimated 4% monthly return, accepting significantly higher potential drawdowns. It leans into leveraged ETFs and concentrated high-conviction names, with a smaller cash buffer and daily rebalancing risk of leveraged products.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs and high-beta single names (SOXL, SPAL, SMH, CRWD).
Trim broad index (SPY) to maximize cash for yield focus.
Significantly increase allocation to income ETF (QQQI) for monthly distributions.
Hold remaining cash to park for yield opportunities.
Avoid new adds to maintain a cash buffer and focus on income.
Prioritize low drawdown and reliable monthly income over growth.
income.read: This income-focused plan targets around 1% monthly return through distributions, prioritizing capital preservation. It eliminates speculative assets, maximizes allocation to income-generating ETFs, and maintains a substantial cash buffer for stability, though NAV can still fall.
